aliases: French breadstick set with hot beverage and eggs
Soft breadstick with coffee and eggs

english: French breadstick set with hot beverage and eggs
Soft breadstick with coffee and eggs

food_subgroup: Bread-based set meal with hot beverage and eggs

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Singaporean kopitiam breadstick set meal. FOODON_00001183 (bread food product) is used as the closest parent category covering the bread component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a composite kopitiam breadstick set with beverage and eggs.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this composite Singaporean kopitiam set meal.

preparation_method: served with boiled or soft-boiled eggs

serving.description: 1 set (2 soft French breadsticks + 2 eggs + 1 M-size hot beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a set comprising two soft French breadsticks (approximately 120 g total), two soft-boiled or half-boiled eggs (approximately 100 g total), and one M-size hot beverage such as kopi or teh (approximately 160 ml, counted at ~160 g), totalling approximately 380 g.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate: HPB Singapore Foodo — Bread (French loaf / baguette), Egg (soft-boiled), and Beverage (kopi/teh) entries; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for French bread and egg nutrient profiles
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exists for the complete Soft French Breadstick Set. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) HPB Foodo entry for French bread / baguette-style bread (~270 kcal/100g, protein 9g, fat 2g, carbohydrate 53g per 100g); (2) USDA SR Legacy entry for soft-boiled egg (~147 kcal/100g, protein 12.6g, fat 9.9g, carbohydrate 0.8g per 100g); (3) HPB Foodo entry for kopi-O or teh-O (minimal caloric contribution from beverage, ~10 kcal/100g). The composite per-100g values are weighted by approximate component mass fractions within the 380g set: bread 120g (31.6%), eggs 100g (26.3%), beverage 160g (42.1%). Weighted composite per 100g is calculated accordingly. The beverage component (kopi or teh with condensed milk) may add sugar and fat; values here assume a standard kopi with condensed milk as served at Toast Box.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — French baguette / white bread entry (GI ~72)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Soft French Breadstick Set as a composite meal. The GI of 72 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 for French baguette / white French bread (GI 72, glucose reference). The egg and beverage components contribute negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (72 × 37.8) / 100 ≈ 27.2, where available carbohydrate per serving = 19.8 g/100g × 380g / 100 ≈ 75.2g total, but the bread fraction (120g at 53g carb/100g = 63.6g carb) dominates; blended set carbohydrate per serving ≈ 75.2g; GL = (72 × 37.8) / 100 ≈ 27.2 using the bread-dominant GI and blended carbohydrate per serving of 37.8g net (accounting for fibre). The GI of the set is dominated by the French bread component.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from bread and beverage
hypertension.guidance: The set provides approximately 798 mg sodium per serving (210 mg/100g × 380g), which represents about 35% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size and request low-sugar or no-condensed-milk beverage options to reduce overall sodium and caloric load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index from refined white French bread and added sugar in beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 72 (high) and a glycemic load of approximately 27 per serving, this set can cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider limiting portion of bread, choosing unsweetened kopi-O or teh-O instead of condensed-milk versions, and pairing with a source of dietary fibre or protein to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol from eggs and saturated fat from condensed milk in beverage
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contributes approximately 437 mg dietary cholesterol (primarily from two eggs) and moderate saturated fat (~6.8 g per serving); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit egg yolk consumption and opt for lower-fat beverage alternatives such as black coffee or plain tea to reduce saturated fat and cholesterol intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Soft French Breadstick Set is a popular Singaporean kopitiam-style breakfast or snack set served at Toast Box outlets, including the Funan branch. The set comprises two soft French breadsticks — a baguette-style white bread with a soft, slightly chewy interior and a lightly crisp exterior — accompanied by two eggs (typically soft-boiled or half-boiled in the Singaporean tradition) and one M-size hot beverage, usually kopi (local Robusta coffee with condensed milk) or teh (local black tea with condensed milk). The breadsticks are baked and served warm, offering a mild, slightly buttery flavour that pairs well with the rich, sweet beverage. Nutritionally, the set is a moderate-to-high calorie meal providing approximately 562 kcal per serving, with meaningful protein from the eggs (~27 g per set), carbohydrates predominantly from the refined white bread (high GI of 72), and fat from both the egg yolks and the condensed milk in the beverage. The sodium content is moderate at approximately 798 mg per serving. Clinically, the high glycemic index of the bread component makes this set a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance, and the cholesterol contribution from two eggs warrants attention for those with cardiovascular disease. The set is halal-certified at Toast Box and is a widely recognised item in Singapore's kopitiam breakfast culture, often consumed in the morning or as an afternoon snack.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for the complete Soft French Breadstick Set as a composite meal. Nutrient values are estimated by combining HPB Singapore Foodo entries for French bread and soft-boiled egg with USDA SR Legacy data, weighted by approximate component mass fractions. The GI is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database for French baguette as the dominant glycemic component. Confidence is medium because the composite estimation introduces uncertainty, particularly regarding the beverage formulation (condensed milk quantity varies by outlet and barista).
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy data; no single approved source entry covers this exact set meal. 2. The beverage component (kopi or teh with condensed milk) is assumed to be a standard M-size (~160 ml) with condensed milk; sugar and fat contributions from the beverage are included in the composite estimate. 3. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for French bread and eggs; condensed milk may contain trace trans fats below detection threshold — treated as 0.0). 4. GI of 72 is from the University of Sydney GI Database for French baguette/white French bread (Atkinson et al. 2021); this is an analogous estimate as no direct GI measurement exists for this composite set. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin values, and mineral values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy weighted composite; these are approximate and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis if precision is required. 6. Halal status is based on Toast Box's publicly stated halal certification in Singapore. 7. Price range SGD 2.40–10.00 is preserved from the field survey source record as contextual metadata only; it is not used as a nutritional source.
